text.title: MVP Scoping Examples
text.summary: Two worked examples — a time-tracking app and a course platform — showing how a 40+ feature list compresses to a 4-item Must-Have scope with explicit learning goals.

example.note: Freelancers forget to track time, losing billable hours. Initial list had 40+ features. Scoped to 4-week MVP.
example.code:
Must Have (4 weeks) | Why --------------------------|----------------------------- Start/stop timer | Core functionality Project selection | Assign time to project Basic time report | See what was tracked Simple invoice export | Get paid
Won't Have - Mobile app (use responsive web) - Team features (solo focus first) - Integrations (manual entry for now) - Advanced reports (spreadsheet export)
Learning goal: Will users actually track time daily? Kill criterion: If < 30% of signups log time on 5+ days in week 2, pivot.

example.note: Course creators struggle with tech setup. Scoped to 6-week MVP targeting one learning goal.
example.code:
Must Have (6 weeks) | Why --------------------------|----------------------------- Upload video/text lessons | Core content delivery Paywall + payment | Must monetize to test willingness-to-pay Student access control | Protect paid content Basic progress tracking | Show value to student
Won't Have - Quizzes (manual feedback loop) - Certificates (v2 feature) - Community features (external Discord) - Mobile app (responsive web) - Advanced analytics (basic counts only)
Learning goal: Will creators pay $29/month for simplicity over feature-rich competitors? Kill criterion: If < 5 paid subscriptions by week 8, pricing or positioning is wrong.

example.note: 10 Must-Have features listed for a 4-week timebox with 1 developer.
example.code: Must Have: auth, billing, dashboard, notifications, onboarding, search, reporting, settings, integrations, mobile app Timebox: 4 weeks | Capacity: 1 dev
